purpose: Filter incoming air by removing particles and contaminants while maintaining adequate airflow to downstream components

outputs.name: filtered_air_flow
outputs.description: Volumetric flow rate of filtered air passing through the filter element

states.name: filter_element_condition
states.description: Filter element degradation due to dust accumulation and clogging

states.name: filter_efficiency
states.description: Filter efficiency degradation due to particles smaller than 2 µm bypassing or damaging filter media

events.name: choked_filter
events.description: Air inlet filter element becomes clogged, restricting airflow

parameters.name: filter_element_lifetime
parameters.description: Expected operational lifetime of filter element under normal dust conditions

parameters.name: filter_efficiency_lifetime
parameters.description: Expected lifetime for filter efficiency degradation under fine particle exposure

parameters.name: dust_accumulation_rate
parameters.description: Rate factor for dust accumulation affecting filter element condition

parameters.name: fine_particle_exposure_rate
parameters.description: Rate factor for fine particle exposure affecting filter efficiency

parameters.name: nominal_air_flow
parameters.description: Nominal air flow rate through clean filter at standard conditions

parameters.name: choked_filter_threshold
parameters.description: Filter element condition threshold below which filter is considered choked

state_transition_logic: filter_element_condition decreases by (dust_accumulation_rate / filter_element_lifetime) * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
filter_efficiency decreases by (fine_particle_exposure_rate / filter_efficiency_lifetime) * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
assumptions: Dust accumulation is the primary mechanism for filter element degradation
Fine particles cause gradual efficiency loss independent of clogging
Air flow reduction is proportional to both element condition and efficiency
Filter operates under constant upstream pressure conditions
No bypass flow occurs around the filter element
